svg.title: Forecasting pipeline — architecture, ensemble weighting, and results

g.text: fixed-weight blend, tuned to minimize backtest WAPE
learns the blend’s residual on backtest folds

g.title: How the ensemble is weighted

g.text: greedy hill-climbing on held-out WAPE — not hand-tuned, not a plain average
optimizes held-out WAPE directly, not squared error
weights stay non-negative and sparse on collinear candidates
Weights normalized at blend time · a missing member drops out
Algorithm: Caruana et al. (2004), “Ensemble selection from libraries of models” · found via Amazon AutoGluon’s weighted-ensemble source

g.title: WAPE waterfall — every component earns its place
g.text: WAPE on 2025-10-26 (leak-free); the held-out backtest-fold mean tells the same story. Lower is better.
